Verifying that Luecken et al. (2022) actually support this exact rank-ordering is central to the section's validity.","R1.5":"The three-paragraph structure is structurally redundant: each paragraph restates the Harmony-ranking premise and the MethodSelector consequence with no net new content. Structural consolidation is demanded regardless of whether the delegation is read as polish or substantive review.","R1.6":"\"Help me with this section\" most naturally invokes prose improvement; sentence-level repetition across the three paragraphs ('Harmony's first-place ranking', 'Harmony's top scIB bio-conservation score', 'Harmony so clearly dominates') is a direct writing-polish target.","R1.7":"The Luecken et al. (2022) citation is deployed to license a precise rank-ordering claim that may not accurately reflect the paper's findings; citation fidelity is directly at stake here."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Do you want a substantive technical review—including verifying whether Luecken et al. (2022) actually rank Harmony first on the bio-conservation axis of scIB—or primarily writing polish and structural tightening that takes the factual claims as given?"}

rationale.R1.2: All three paragraphs repeat the same inferential chain (Harmony tops scIB bio-conservation → MethodSelector defaults to it) without additive logical content; the structure is circular and the reasoning tautological, making consistency checking necessary under any plausible interpretation of the delegation.
rationale.R1.3: The section's entire architectural justification rests on a single specific empirical claim—that Harmony is the top-ranked method on the bio-conservation axis of the scIB benchmark—which is asserted three times as settled fact. Verifying that Luecken et al. (2022) actually support this exact rank-ordering is central to the section's validity.
rationale.R1.5: The three-paragraph structure is structurally redundant: each paragraph restates the Harmony-ranking premise and the MethodSelector consequence with no net new content. Structural consolidation is demanded regardless of whether the delegation is read as polish or substantive review.
rationale.R1.6: "Help me with this section" most naturally invokes prose improvement; sentence-level repetition across the three paragraphs ('Harmony's first-place ranking', 'Harmony's top scIB bio-conservation score', 'Harmony so clearly dominates') is a direct writing-polish target.
rationale.R1.7: The Luecken et al. (2022) citation is deployed to license a precise rank-ordering claim that may not accurately reflect the paper's findings; citation fidelity is directly at stake here.

parsed.clarification_question: Do you want a substantive technical review—including verifying whether Luecken et al. (2022) actually rank Harmony first on the bio-conservation axis of scIB—or primarily writing polish and structural tightening that takes the factual claims as given?
